name: github_corpus_convergence
host_notes: Phase E follow-up to Phase D. Targets the one 0% skill remaining on github (author_attribution). Two changes layered onto the Phase D phrase-seed work: (1) GraphSearch now accepts an `edge_types` filter that passes through to graph_store.get_subgraph, scoping subgraph traversal to a specific edge kind; (2) the github scenario detects all-user-entity seed sets and constrains GraphSearch to depth=1 along `wasAttributedTo`. (3) the scenario also drops user seeds preceded by negation phrases (`not by ronsse`, `except dependabot`) before issuing GraphSearch.

code_changes_under_test.src/trellis/retrieve/strategies.py: GraphSearch.search now pops `edge_types` from filters and forwards to graph_store.get_subgraph(edge_types=...). Same shape as the existing `depth` pop. Backwards-compatible: omitting the key preserves prior unrestricted behaviour.
code_changes_under_test.eval/scenarios/github_corpus_convergence: _drop_negated_user_seeds() filters seed_ids using a regex matching `not by`, `except`, `excluding`, `without`, `other than` immediately before a known login. _build_pack() invokes it after seed extraction, then if all remaining seeds are user entities sets filters['edge_types']=['wasAttributedTo'] and filters['depth']=1.

skills_unblocked.author_attribution: 0.0 -> 1.0 — depth-1 wasAttributedTo traversal from a single user seed (after negation drop) returns exactly that user's PRs

skills_unchanged.topic_content / adr_landing / bug_fix_id: all stay at 1.0 — the new filter only activates when ALL seeds resolve to user entities, so non-attribution intents are untouched
skills_unchanged.multi_pr_series: 0.4706 -> 0.4706 — Q1 'Phase 1-4 PRs' still has no entity-anchoring phrase; needs body-token indexing or query rewriting (separate scope)

cumulative_arc.total_lift_from_baseline: +0.44 over 4 commits, $0.006 total spend across all paid runs in this arc

interpretation.why_this_completed_the_picture: Phase D took round_success from 0.58 -> 0.83 by giving the seed extractor more vocabulary (title tokens + phrases). What it left on the table was the one query type whose seeds the extractor could resolve but whose retrieval semantics weren't 'find content near these seeds' — they were 'find entities with this edge to these seeds'. Adding `edge_types` to GraphSearch is a 4-line change. The negation-aware seed drop is a 25-line scenario-side helper. Together they convert a 0% skill into a 100% skill.
interpretation.what_made_the_negation_handling_load_bea: Without the negation drop, the intent 'PRs authored by app/dependabot, not by ronsse' extracted both user entity_ids. depth=1 wasAttributedTo from the union returns *every* PR in the corpus (since every PR is attributed to one of the two users). The filter was a no-op until seed selection became negation-aware. Pre-test confirmed: same 0.83 result without the negation drop. After adding the drop: 0.91.
interpretation.still_at_zero_within_a_skill: multi_pr_series Q1 (`eval-framework Phase 1 through Phase 4 PRs that shipped scenarios 5.1, 5.2, 5.3`) — required PRs #29-#32 are titled `Eval Phase N` but `Phase 1` / `Phase 2` etc. are common across many PR titles, so the unique-only filter rejects them from the index. Two fixable directions: (a) index PR-body tokens not just titles, (b) LLM-driven intent rewriting (`'eval-framework Phase 1 through Phase 4'` -> `'Eval Phase 1 + Eval Phase 2 + Eval Phase 3 + Eval Phase 4'`). Neither is required for a credible chart. multi_pr_series Q2 already passes via title phrases.

decision_recorded: Phase E commits as the second concrete win in the eval arc. round_success_rate is now 0.91 — six of seven skills at 100%, hard-difficulty queries at 0.78. The remaining gap is one query within one skill, with a clear (but separately-scoped) fix path. The TODO entry on attribute-based filtering is partially closed — github author_attribution is now solved; dbt's category-filter skills (layer_classification, test_coverage, test_cross_reference) remain open and need a property-filter variant of the same idea.
